records.ety: [L. quaestor, contr. fr. quaesitor, fr. quaerere, quaesitum, to seek for, ask: cf. F. questeur.]

defs: An officer who had the management of the public treasure; a receiver of taxes, tribute, etc.; treasurer of state.
notes: ☞ At an early period there were also public accusers styled questors, but the office was soon abolished.
